</details>

**Handle heartbeat write failures inside the interval callback.**

At line 55, `reply.raw.write(...)` is unguarded inside `setInterval`; write errors can escape the callback and crash the process. The codebase already demonstrates this pattern in `broad

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Refresh realized P&L when `applyPartialFill()` closes the row.**

When `quantity - filledQuantity <= 0`, this helper now sets `status = 'closed'` and `closed_at`, but it still leaves `pnl_usd` and `pnl_ratio` untouched. The cancelled/expired late-fill path in `monitorOrders()` can close a position through this function, so the row can keep stale mark-to-market metrics instead of the realized totals. Mirror the `closeStockPosition()` calculation

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ | _⚡ Quick win_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# First, let's understand the file structure and size
wc -l src/execution/order-manager.ts
```

Repository: MeNass89/stock-tracker

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Read the critical section around lines 180-187
sed -n '170,200p' src/execution/order-manager.ts
```

Repository: MeNass89/stock-tracker

Length of output: 1460

---

🏁 Script execu

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Do not stack discretionary exits on top of live stop orders.**

After `stopLossFilled()` returns `false`, this block can still arm a trailing stop on Line 59 and then immediately queue `sellHalf()`/`exit()`, or submit a market exit while `stopLossOrderId`/`trailingStopOrderId` is still working. That creates overlapping sell orders for the same shares. Bail out while either stop order id is present, or cancel the resting stop before any discr

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Treat whole-share stop fills as partial when a fractional remainder exists.**

`submitStopLoss()` and `activateTrailingStop()` only place `Math.floor(position.quantity)` shares, but this path always calls `closeStockPosition()`. If a 1-share stop fills against a 1.5-share position, the row is marked `closed` while 0.5 shares are still open. Use `applyPartialFill()` when `filledQty < position.quantity`, and only full-close when the remainder

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ | _🏗️ Heavy lift_

**Keep failed rebalance claims durable.**

`clearRebalanceRun()` here deletes the claim even after `executeDiffs()` may already have submitted some sells or buys. A retry will replay the same filing from the top and can double-submit live orders. Use a durable `failed`/`in_progress` state or checkpointed recovery instead of deleting the row once external side effects have started.

As per coding guidelines, `src/execution/**`: "This handles
